title: First Unreal Project Setup
instructions: Create a new Unreal Engine project using the class naming convention.
requiredSteps: Open Unreal Engine through the approved class workflow.
Create a new project using the assigned template and starter content setting.
Name the project using the class naming convention.
Open the project and confirm that it saves in the correct location.
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the open Unreal project
Screenshot or written confirmation showing the correct project name

rubric.description: Complete, polished, and shows strong attention to naming, setup, and evidence.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but not functional or unclear.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Create one extra saved copy or screenshot showing where your project files are located.
reflectionPrompt: What part of project setup do you need to remember next time?

title: Unreal Interface Map
instructions: Create an interface map that identifies the major Unreal editor areas and what each one is used for.
requiredSteps: Open the Unreal project from Lesson 1.
Capture a screenshot of the editor interface.
Label the Viewport, Outliner, Details panel, Content Drawer, toolbar, and play controls.
Write a short purpose statement for each labeled area.
evidenceRequired: Annotated screenshot of the Unreal interface
Short notes describing the purpose of key panels

rubric.description: Complete, polished, and shows strong attention to naming, setup, and evidence.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but not functional or unclear.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Add one additional panel or menu that was not required and explain when you might use it.
reflectionPrompt: Which interface area do you expect to use most often?

title: Viewport Navigation Scavenger Hunt
instructions: Navigate through the starter scene and capture evidence that you can view objects from multiple useful angles.
requiredSteps: Use orbit, pan, zoom, and focus controls.
Find the assigned objects or scene locations.
Capture screenshots from the required angles.
Write one sentence naming a shortcut or control that helped you.
evidenceRequired: Short note describing one navigation shortcut used

rubric.description: Complete, polished, and shows strong attention to naming, setup, and evidence.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but not functional or unclear.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Record a short screen capture showing smooth navigation from one object to another.
reflectionPrompt: Which navigation movement felt most natural, and which still needs practice?

title: Transform Practice Scene
instructions: Build a simple practice scene that shows control over moving, rotating, and scaling objects.
requiredSteps: Place several starter objects in the scene.
Move, rotate, and scale objects intentionally.
Use snapping or typed transform values for at least one object.
Capture a screenshot and include one transform value example.
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of a practice scene built with transformed objects
One written example of a location, rotation, or scale value

rubric.description: Complete, polished, and shows strong attention to naming, setup, and evidence.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but not functional or unclear.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Arrange objects into a recognizable structure or pathway.
reflectionPrompt: Which transform tool was easiest to control, and why?

title: Q3 Production Project Setup
instructions: Set up the Q3 Unreal production project and define a clear goal for the castle environment.
requiredSteps: Create or open the assigned Q3 Unreal project.
Set up project folders for planning, screenshots, and production notes.
Write a short production goal for the castle environment.
Capture project setup evidence.
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the Q3 project setup
Written production goal for the castle environment

rubric.description: Complete, polished, and shows strong attention to naming, setup, and evidence.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but not functional or unclear.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Add a simple naming system for screenshots and production notes.
reflectionPrompt: What production habit will help you finish a stronger capstone?

title: Castle Environment Plan
instructions: Create a plan for a fantasy/castle environment using references, constraints, and a clear focal point.
requiredSteps: Collect teacher-approved visual references.
Identify the environment mood and main focal point.
Sketch or describe the basic layout.
List constraints or assets you expect to use.
evidenceRequired: Simple layout sketch or written scene plan

rubric.description: Complete, polished, and shows strong attention to naming, setup, and evidence.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but not functional or unclear.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Add a color or mood reference and explain how it should affect lighting later.
reflectionPrompt: What do you want the viewer to understand about this world?

title: Third-Person Walkthrough Setup
instructions: Set up a third-person project or template configuration so the castle environment can be tested from player view.
requiredSteps: Create or configure the third-person project.
Confirm the playable character can move in the level.
Check that the environment scale feels appropriate.
Capture a screenshot showing the player character.
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the playable character in the project
Short note explaining why player scale matters

rubric.description: Complete, polished, and shows strong attention to naming, setup, and evidence.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but not functional or unclear.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Mark one intended player route through the future castle environment.
reflectionPrompt: What does the player view reveal that the editor camera might hide?

title: Castle Landscape Blockout
instructions: Create a rough terrain blockout that supports the castle environment's focal point and playable route.
requiredSteps: Create or open the landscape area.
Sculpt rough landforms for the castle, route, and surrounding terrain.
Check the blockout from top-down and player-view angles.
Capture screenshots showing the plan.
evidenceRequired: Top-down screenshot of the landscape blockout
Player-view screenshot showing the route or focal point

rubric.description: Complete, polished, and shows strong attention to naming, setup, and evidence.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but not functional or unclear.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Add one temporary marker showing where the castle entrance should be.
reflectionPrompt: What terrain choice makes the castle feel more intentional?
